meta.description: Evaluate clinical net benefit of diagnostic or prognostic models across probability thresholds using DCA. Inputs: predicted probabilities, binary outcomes. Outputs: DCA curve plot, net benefit table, threshold comparison.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated scientific claims, invented numeric outputs, or unsupported conclusions were observed across the audit inputs.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed inside research-analysis scope and did not provide diagnosis or treatment advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflow uses an appropriate binary logistic DCA design for decision-curve and clinical-impact evaluation.

code_usability.detail: Valid runs were executable and invalid inputs failed cleanly with actionable SKILL_* errors.

functional_suitability.note: The skill covers the promised binary clinical DCA workflow with aligned CLI, outputs, references, and bundled test data.

reliability.note: Validation, deterministic execution, and reproducibility logging are strong, though overwrite protection currently triggers only after the model has already been fit.

performance_context.note: Progressive disclosure is clear and the top-level contract stays concise for a moderate-complexity analysis skill.

agent_usability.note: The trigger boundaries, runnable examples, error table, and bundled smoke test make the skill easy to invoke correctly.

human_usability.note: The workflow is easy to follow and all observable runtime messaging now conforms to the skill's standardized logging style.

security.note: Inputs are validated, no unsafe execution primitives are present, and all file handling remains local and explicit.

maintainability.note: Responsibilities are cleanly separated across CLI, orchestration, analysis helpers, and utilities, with no direct file I/O left in functions.R.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision, progressive disclosure, boundary handling, and testability are all strong; the skill could still tighten failure-short-circuit behavior on occupied output directories.

inputs.label: Default bundled DCA run
inputs.note: Completed successfully and produced the documented data, table, plot, and metadata files while keeping all observable runtime output inside the standardized logging format.

assertions.text: The documented main CLI path is runnable as described.
assertions.note: Rscript scripts/main.R completed successfully with the bundled dataset.

assertions.text: The run emits the documented data, table, plot, and metadata artifacts.
assertions.note: dca_model.rds, dca_summary.txt, decision_curve.pdf, clinical_impact_curve.pdf, and session_info.txt were present.

assertions.text: The workflow records reproducibility metadata.
assertions.note: session_info.txt captured parameters, the fixed seed, and session/package details.

assertions.text: The output stays within the stated decision-curve-analysis scope.
assertions.note: The run produced DCA-specific artifacts only, without ROC, calibration, or survival outputs.

inputs.label: Cohort mode + custom styling
inputs.note: Accepted valid cohort-mode, CI, standardization, threshold, and styling overrides while preserving the standard output bundle.

assertions.text: The skill accepts valid cohort-mode and styling overrides.
assertions.note: The cohort design, threshold step, CI flag, standardization flag, colors, and title all parsed successfully.

assertions.text: The output bundle remains stable under supported customization.
assertions.note: The same standard data, table, plot, and metadata outputs were still created.

assertions.text: Runtime metadata preserves the chosen overrides.
assertions.note: session_info.txt recorded the custom design, threshold grid size, plot colors, and title.

assertions.text: The customized run remains inside the skill's stated purpose.
assertions.note: The workflow still performed binary decision-curve analysis only.

inputs.label: Missing-column rejection
inputs.note: Rejected the missing predictor request with a clear SKILL_MISSING_COLUMNS error before any audit output directory was created.

assertions.text: The skill rejects a missing predictor column before model fitting.
assertions.note: Execution stopped during analysis-data preparation.

assertions.text: The failure message is specific and actionable.
assertions.note: The error names the missing column directly.

assertions.text: The failure does not emit misleading analytical artifacts.
assertions.note: No audit output directory or result files were created for the invalid run.

assertions.text: The boundary behavior matches the documented contract.
assertions.note: The rejection is consistent with the troubleshooting guide and error table.

inputs.label: Documented smoke-test helper
inputs.note: The documented smoke-test helper executed successfully and rebuilt the expected output directory with the full artifact set.

assertions.text: The R smoke-test helper is runnable as documented.
assertions.note: Rscript tests/run_smoke_test.R executed successfully.

assertions.text: The helper rebuilds the documented output bundle.
assertions.note: tests/output contained the expected data, plot, table, and session files after execution.

assertions.text: The helper preserves reproducibility metadata.
assertions.note: tests/output/session_info.txt was regenerated and remained non-empty.

assertions.text: The helper stays inside the skill's normal operational scope.
assertions.note: It simply wraps the main DCA workflow with bundled data.

inputs.label: Overwrite guard
inputs.note: The first seeded run succeeded and the second rerun was blocked cleanly by overwrite protection, although the guard triggers after model fitting rather than before analysis begins.

assertions.text: The skill prevents silent replacement of an existing result bundle.
assertions.note: The second invocation was blocked before any files in the existing bundle were replaced.

assertions.text: The overwrite failure is specific and actionable.
assertions.note: The error message explicitly instructs the operator to use --overwrite.

assertions.text: The initial successful run still produced a complete audit trail.
assertions.note: The first invocation created the expected artifact bundle and session_info.txt.

assertions.text: The protection mechanism stays inside the skill's documented operating model.
assertions.note: It adds safety without changing the DCA workflow semantics.

key_strengths: The skill now matches the target data-analysis structure closely, including a standards-aligned CLI, references, test data, and reproducibility logging.
The execution contract is strong: valid runs complete, missing-column failures are controlled, and the smoke-test helper is runnable.
The refactor removed direct file I/O from functions.R, which now better matches the required role split between orchestration and core analysis logic.
The workflow keeps clear scope boundaries around binary decision-curve analysis and avoids leaking into unrelated ROC, calibration, or survival tasks.

recommendations.title: Short-circuit overwrite checks before fitting
recommendations.problem: The rerun into an existing non-empty output directory still fits the DCA model before failing on overwrite protection, which wastes time and compute.
recommendations.root_cause: create_output_dirs() is called after model fitting in run_analysis().
recommendations.fix: Check or reserve the output directory before expensive analysis starts, or split the overwrite validation from the directory-creation step so occupied directories fail fast.
